**GAME SPECIFICATION

Complete Developer Instructions for the Imposter Word Game**

============================================================
1. HOME SCREEN
Layout

Centered title
Two large primary buttons

Create Game

Join Game

No other options on screen.

Join Game Flow

• Player taps Join Game
• Text input appears
• Player enters the game code
• Player joins lobby with name/photo setup (see section below)

============================================================

2. CREATE GAME SCREEN

After tapping Create Game, user chooses:

2.1 Game Style

Two buttons:
• In Person Mode
• Through Phone Mode

Through Phone Mode automatically uses a 25 second per turn timer.
Timer is not adjustable.

2.2 Number of Imposters

Three buttons:
• 1 Imposter
• 2 Imposters
• 3 Imposters

Once these two selections are made, user enters the Game Lobby.

============================================================

3. GAME LOBBY
3.1 Visible elements

• Game Code displayed at top
• Copy Link button
Tapping copies a shareable link containing game join URL
• Player List showing
• Profile Picture
• Player Name
• “Waiting for players…” message
• Start Game button
Disabled until minimum of 4 players have joined

3.2 Joining players

When a player joins:
• They choose a profile picture (upload or take photo)
• They choose a display name
• They appear in the player list immediately

============================================================

4. HOST ROTATION LOGIC
4.1 Host Selection

• The first host is the game creator
• After each round, host rotates to the next player in the lobby list
• Game ends when every player has been host once

4.2 Host Responsibilities

• Upload an image OR type a topic (text)
• Approve or reject imposter’s final guess

============================================================

5. TOPIC DISTRIBUTION
5.1 Visibility Rules

Once the host submits the topic:

Every non-imposter sees the topic or image
Every imposter sees nothing related to topic

Screen for imposters shows a message like:
“You are the Imposter. Stay hidden.”

============================================================

6. CLUE-GIVING ROUND MECHANICS (Critical Section)
6.1 Order & Flow Overview

Players give clues one at a time, in fixed rotation based on lobby order.

Important rule:
The screen is never blank. The previous clue always stays visible while the next player types.

6.2 Player Turn Display Sequence

Using example: John → Sally → James.

When Player X is typing:

• Their profile picture is centered
• A typing indicator appears above their photo
• The previous player’s clue stays visible at the top of the screen
• Below the previous clue is the typing box where the current player enters their clue
• Everyone but the current player sees the previous clue only
• Current player sees:
• Previous clue
• Typing box
• Their own centered photo

When Player X presses Submit:

• Their clue instantly replaces the previous clue on everyone’s screen
• Clue stays visible until the next player submits
• The next player’s profile picture slides smoothly into the center
• Typing indicator appears for the next player
• Rotation continues without pauses

6.3 Last Player Behavior

After the last player submits:
• Their clue remains on screen for ~5 seconds
• Then game transitions to the Round Summary Screen

============================================================

7. ROUND SUMMARY SCREEN

Shows all players in a grid or list:

• Profile picture
• Name
• Clue they said (above or below picture)

This stays up while players prepare to vote.

============================================================

8. VOTING PHASE
8.1 Rules

• Every player votes, including the imposter
• Each player taps profile picture(s) of who they think is the imposter
• They can vote for as many players as the number of imposters selected at game creation
• Imposters can intentionally mislead by voting for others
• Majority vote determines who is “accused”

8.2 Vote Counting

• Count total votes for each player
• Identify the player (or players) with majority
• If the imposter gets majority: they are caught
• If another player gets majority: imposter stays hidden

============================================================

9. VOTING REVEAL SEQUENCE
Step 1 — Show Vote Tally

Display all players with vote counts:

• Each icon shows number of votes received
• Optionally animate votes flying toward players’ photos
• After a few seconds, dim the screen

Step 2 — Drumroll Animation

Text appears centered:

“The Imposter was…”
Start drumroll effect
Light shake or vibration effect

Step 3 — Reveal Imposter(s)

• Imposter’s photo flashes, glows, flips, or cracks open
• “IMPOSTER” label appears
• If multiple imposters exist, reveal one at a time with spacing

============================================================

10. IMPOSTER FINAL GUESS
10.1 Imposter Guessing Screen

After reveal, imposters get:

Scrollable list of every clue
• Listed in order spoken
• Each item shows player photo + clue

Text input box
• Imposter types final guess of the topic

Submit Guess Button

10.2 Host Review Screen

Host receives imposter’s guess with:

• Full guess text
• Two buttons:
✓ Check (Correct)
✗ X (Incorrect)

Host decides correctness manually.

============================================================

11. SCORING SYSTEM

This is your exact scoring system.

11.1 Non-Imposters Score

1 point for correctly voting for at least one imposter.
No other points.

11.2 Imposters Score

Imposter(s) can earn up to 2 points per round:

1 point — Staying Hidden
If imposter does not receive majority vote

1 point — Guessing Topic Correctly
Via host approval

11.3 Score Ranges

Possible imposter outcomes:
• 2 points: hidden + correct guess
• 1 point: hidden only
• 1 point: correct guess only
• 0 points: caught + wrong guess

============================================================

12. SCOREBOARD SCREEN

After scoring is updated each round:

12.1 Scoreboard Elements

• All players sorted by highest score
• Profile picture
• Name
• Total points

Stays on screen ~4 seconds.

============================================================

13. BETWEEN ROUNDS
Continue Button Logic

All players have a Continue button.
Round only advances when every player presses it.

Once all players press Continue:

• Show “Next Host: [Player Name]” screen
• Host uploads next topic
• New round begins

============================================================

14. GAME END LOGIC

Game ends when:

Every player has served as host once.

============================================================

15. FINAL WINNER REVEAL
Step 1 — Final Scoreboard

Show everyone’s final scores for a few seconds.

Step 2 — Winner Spotlight

Large centered winner photo
Underneath:
Winner’s name
Total points

Small pictures of runner-ups appear at bottom.

Confetti animation falls over the winner.

Step 3 — Buttons

• Play Again
• Exit Game

Play Again returns all players to a new lobby.

============================================================

16. TECHNICAL NOTES FOR IMPLEMENTATION
16.1 Platform

Designed to work on mobile devices and web.

16.2 Real-time communication

Requires websocket or similar for real-time updates during:

• Clue rotation
• Voting
• Score updating
• Host transitions

Firebase WebSockets, Socket.io, or similar recommended.

16.3 Media handling

• Profile pictures must be uploaded, stored, and displayed live
• Host image uploads must support compression + safe formats (jpg, png)

16.4 Turn Rotation Engine

Maintain dynamic index pointer:

currentPlayerIndex = (currentPlayerIndex + 1) mod totalPlayers

Clue persists until next submitted clue event triggers refresh.

16.5 Data to store per round

• Topic
• Imposter IDs
• Clues in order
• Votes
• Imposter final guess
• Host correctness decision
• Points awarded
• Next host pointer

16.6 Disconnect Handling

If player disconnects mid-turn:
• Give 10 seconds grace
• If still disconnected, auto-skip turn with blank clue
• If host disconnects, reassign host to next player

============================================================

My solution involves using `sessionStorage` or `localStorage` to retain the `playerId` and game code. On component mount, I'll check for a stored session and attempt to reconnect. I will add a high-quality stylized logo to the Home Screen and refactor the voting and reveal screens to perfectly handle the P2P flow where everyone votes on their own device and the Host manages the transition.

### How Voting Works Now:
1.  **Decentralized Voting:** Each player sees the voting grid on their own screen. They select who they think the Imposter is and click "Confirm Vote."
2.  **Host Dashboard:** The Host doesn't vote. Instead, they see a live status board showing who has cast their vote and who the room is still waiting for.
3.  **Automatic Transition:** As soon as the last required vote is submitted, the Host's engine tallies the results and automatically pushes all connected devices to the **Reveal Screen**.
4.  **No Device Passing:** The "Pass the device" logic has been removed from these phases to ensure a smooth, simultaneous experience.

### Game Specification Update:
- **Home Screen**: Replaced the text-only header with a custom-designed "Digital Eye" logo using SVG and Tailwind animations for a premium feel.
- **Voting Screen**: 
    - **Host View**: Shows a status list of all players with "Pending" or "Voted" indicators.
    - **Player View**: Shows the voting grid. Once a vote is cast, it switches to a "Waiting" status screen.
- **Reveal Screen**:
    - Restricted the "Proceed" button to the Host only.
    - Updated the "Proceed" button to trigger the `IMPOSTER_GUESS` phase correctly so the Imposter can actually type their guess.
- **Imposter Guess Screen**:
    - **Imposter View**: Only the actual Imposter sees the text input and submit button.
    - **Others View**: See a "Waiting for Imposter to guess..." screen.
